llcFilesWith: Wyoming Secretary of State (Application for Registration of Trade Name)
soleProprietorFilesWith: Wyoming Secretary of State (the same statewide trade name registration applies to individuals and sole proprietors)
feeBasis: The Secretary of State fee to register a trade name is $100, and renewal is $50.
renewalRule: A trade name registration is effective for ten years from registration or renewal; the Secretary of State sends a renewal notice in the year before expiration, and a $50 renewal application with a statement that the name is still in use keeps it in force.
protectionNote: Wyoming trade name registration is a public claim to the name; the Secretary of State will not register a trade name that is not distinguishable from one already registered, but the registration itself is not a trademark and confers no general exclusive market right.
searchNote: Registered trade names appear in the Secretary of State's business filing search.
notes: Wyoming registers trade names statewide with the Secretary of State (W.S. Title 40, ch. 2). Both LLCs and sole proprietors file for $100; the registration lasts an unusually long ten years and renews for $50. There is no publication requirement, and registration is notice rather than a trademark, though the Secretary of State will refuse a trade name that is not distinguishable from one already on file.

sources.title: Trade Names Registration — Duration and renewal (10 years)
